input.subject: Mathematics
input.reasonContext: The student has been absent for multiple consecutive school days. Express genuine concern for the student's well-being, ask if everything is okay at home, and invite the parent to reach out. Be warm, not accusatory.
input.teacherNote: Aarav has not attended school since Monday. His classmates mentioned he was unwell last week. We miss him in class and want to make sure everything is alright.

input.subject: Science
input.reasonContext: The student's academic performance has recently declined. Focus on offering support — mention that you want to work together to help the student succeed. Do NOT blame the student or parent.
input.teacherNote: Meera is attentive in class but her recent unit test scores have dipped. I would like to arrange extra practice worksheets and a short weekly doubt-clearing session for her.
input.performanceSummary: Science Unit Test 2: 14/40 (35%), Science Mid-term Examination: 23/50 (46%)

input.subject: English
input.reasonContext: Share a positive achievement or behavior. This message should be warm, celebratory, and brief. Encourage the parent to praise the student at home.
input.teacherNote: Rohan recited a poem confidently at the morning assembly in front of the whole school, and later helped a new student settle into the class. His confidence has grown wonderfully this term.
input.schoolName: Zilla Parishad Upper Primary School, Nashik
